working_on: Close #152 via Cluster 2 ADR + migration + bundled soft-warn; re-scope #151 (server-side clean) and add TCP keepalive to psycopg connects
declared_scope: Land (1) Phase 1 — TCP keepalive on validate.py + apply_migration.py psycopg.connect calls + re-scope Issue #151 (server-side logs clean; client-side hang framing) + ops-doc note; (2) Phase 2 — product ADR 0098 (Tier-A Cluster 2: edge-type taxonomy expansion + three-relation layered separation) + migration 0130 + paired rollback + ROUTING.md / PREDICATE_MANIFEST.md / ADR 0061 in-body updates; (3) Phase 3 — `validate_edges_contestability_unguarded_high_confidence` soft-warn implementation per #152 + Cluster 2's `edge_provisional_hard_prerequisite` soft-warn + pytests + ops-doc + close #152. phase: NA-engine-and-product
outcome_summary: Three-phase session: (1) Issue #151 re-scoped after mcp__supabase__get_logs proved server-side clean across the wedge window; TCP keepalive landed on all 5 psycopg.connect sites with 5 regression pytests; observation window opened (criterion #4). (2) Tier-A Cluster 2 — product ADR 0098 + migration 0130 + paired rollback (round-trip verified); re-verifies ADR 0094 T1-A + ADR 0095 T1-A; 533 edges retyped to per-layer vocabulary. (3) Two validator soft-warns landed (#152 + ADR 0098 deliverable); PREREQUISITE_EDGE_TYPES tuple rename fixed 8 silently-broken check sites. Issue #152 closed. Surprises: TCP keepalive mitigation falsified mid-Phase-3 by an in-session wedge that workstation-restart (not Supabase-restart) cleared — user-hypothesized cross-process Python contamination is the next investigation vector for #151. #152's ~17-fire estimate was wrong: warn fires on all 533 because Cluster 2 retyped edge_type not expert_confidence; Cluster 7 SQA work is the natural successor. Three drawers paired (decisions f6b547da / pushback 1d1b2170 / lesson c8f90de5); diary 1731842c. Cold-review (Python + SQL) both clean.

scope_delivery.explanation: All three phases of the approved plan landed: Phase 1 (TCP keepalive + Issue #151 re-scope); Phase 2 (ADR 0098 + migration 0130 + paired rollback + ROUTING.md/PREDICATE_MANIFEST.md/ADR 0061+0094+0095 in-body amendments); Phase 3 (#152 soft-warn + ADR 0098 soft-warn + PREREQUISITE_EDGE_TYPES tuple + pytests). One in-session user-confirmed addition: PREREQUISITE_EDGE_TYPES tuple rename was not in the original plan but became necessary when the Phase 2 commit's validator output showed orphan_leaf:380 (the hardcoded constant silently broke after migration 0130 retyped). This was a fix-in-context per CLAUDE.md discipline. Round-trip rollback verification (deferred per plan, accepted user OK mid-session via AskUserQuestion) also landed.

side_discoveries.reasoning: ADR 0098 in-body prose names L1.10 (node-type partial coverage) as deferred to Cluster 4 per the in-session AskUserQuestion adjudication. Cluster 4 is the next-session work item declared in engine/STATE.md S-0209 row; the deferral is structurally routed to the natural-next-session that ADR 0094 dependency order names. No additional surface required; STATE.md's next-session block carries the forward-pointer.
